validation_gaps: No test covers /antigravity/models response shape ([]ClaudeModel vs []string regression).
No test covers PricedCandidates() scope — returns all-platform catalog instead of antigravity-only set.
No test covers GeminiV1BetaListModels FilterClientFacing call path (design §2.5 not implemented).
pricing_catalog_membership_tk.go and pricing_availability_predicate_tk.go lack isolated unit tests for nil-safety and boundary conditions.

findings.description: AntigravityModels() now returns data: []string when PricedCandidates() is non-empty, but the established contract (and the fallback path) returns data: []ClaudeModel{id, type, display_name, created_at}. This is a breaking response-shape change on GET /antigravity/models — any client that parses the model-list as an object array will receive bare strings instead. The two code paths diverge: the new path serializes as ["model-id", ...] while the fallback still serializes as [{"id":"...","type":"model",...}]. This was not described as a contract change in the design doc.
findings.suggested_fix:
Wrap PricedCandidates() output into []antigravity.ClaudeModel before responding, matching the existing shape:
  priced := h.tkModelListFilter.PricedCandidates()
  models := make([]antigravity.ClaudeModel, 0, len(priced))
  for _, id := range priced {
    models = append(models, antigravity.ClaudeModel{ID: id, Type: "model", DisplayName: id, CreatedAt: "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"})
  }
  c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"object": "list", "data": models})
This preserves the response shape contract while still applying the pricing filter.

findings.description: PricedCandidates() calls PricedModels() which returns the entire pricing catalog (all platforms: claude, openai, gemini, newapi, antigravity models). When AntigravityModels() calls PricedCandidates() it receives models like 'gpt-4o', 'gemini-2.5-flash', etc. — not only the antigravity-compatible subset. The endpoint is supposed to return models Antigravity can serve. Returning a mixed cross-platform catalog to a Claude SDK client that is routing through Antigravity is semantically incorrect and would cause downstream scheduling failures when a client picks a non-antigravity model from the list.
findings.suggested_fix:
PricedCandidates() should accept a platform parameter so callers can scope it:
  func (f *ModelListFilter) PricedCandidates(platform string) []string
Then AntigravityModels() passes the correct platform ("antigravity" or a predicate that covers both claude + gemini via antigravity). Alternatively, filter the candidates through antigravity.DefaultModels() IDs first (intersect instead of replace).

findings.description: Design doc §2.5 explicitly calls for GeminiV1BetaListModels to receive a thin 1-line FilterClientFacing injection. The current implementation has zero filter calls: (1) the upstream proxy path returns writeUpstreamResponse(c, res) unfiltered, (2) the FallbackModelsList() paths (lines 61, 74) are returned unfiltered. Both the antigravity-mode path (FallbackGeminiModelsList) and the no-account fallback (FallbackModelsList) are unfiltered. The design doc's statement that 'FallbackModelsList 路径不动' only exempts the fallback from going away, not from being filtered.
findings.suggested_fix: For the proxy path: filtering the raw Gemini response is non-trivial (structured Gemini model format); acceptable to defer. For the fallback paths, extract model IDs from FallbackModelsList(), run FilterClientFacing, then rebuild the Gemini model struct slice — same pattern as tkClaudeDefaultModelIDs in gateway_handler_tk_model_list.go. This is low-effort (3–5 lines) and brings the implementation in line with the design doc.

findings.description: pricing_catalog_membership_tk.go (IsModelPriced, PricedModels) and pricing_availability_predicate_tk.go (IsUnreachable) have no dedicated unit tests. Both are load-bearing predicates used by DiscoveryFilter.Apply and FilterClientFacing. IsModelPriced's nil-receiver path, empty-modelID guard, and cold-catalog (no models) behavior are untested in isolation. IsUnreachable's nil-repo and PG-error fail-open behaviors are similarly untested. They are covered indirectly via discover_filter_tk_test.go stubs, but isolation tests would catch regressions in the predicate logic itself.
findings.suggested_fix: Add pricing_catalog_membership_tk_test.go covering: nil receiver → false, empty modelID → false, cold catalog → false, priced model → true. Add pricing_availability_predicate_tk_test.go covering: nil receiver → false, repo error → false (fail-open), unreachable status → true, ok status → false.
